title: hypericum scabrum var. asperum
intro: Hypericum scabrum var. asperum, also known as the rough-leaved St. John's Wort, is a plant species belonging to the Hypericum genus, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and woody stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities, making it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Hypericum scabrum var. asperum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, and is often used in folk medicine to treat a range of health issues, including fever, anxiety, and digestive problems.
